prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: Johnny Cash’s cover of Hurt. The stripped-back arrangement and his weathered voice turn a song about self-destruction into an end-of-life reckoning; every crack and breath feels like evidence, stripping away myth and leaving only consequence. Rick Rubin’s unadorned production gives the words room to fall with terrible weight, so even simple phrases land like verdicts. By the final refrain, it feels less like a performance than a confession, and that plainspoken honesty lingers long after the last chord.
